title: Checkout as guest on iOS does not send email and nor show up in dashboard.
author: imranAfzal43A
body:
We are building REACT NATIVE EXPO for Android and iOS using the Bagisto rest api. 
Using the app as a guest and paying via Stripe works perfectly on Android. The user places the order and gets an email; we can also view that order in the dashboard.
While on iOS api works perfectly and gives a success response, but the user does not get an email for the order, and we cannot view the order in the dashboard either.

title: Introduce Native Shipping Zones with Region-Based Shipping Methods and Location Matching (Similar to WooCommerce)
author: skm15187
body: ### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. Bagisto currently provides shipping method configuration and shipping origin settings, but it lacks a native Shipping Zone management system. As a result, merchants cannot define geographical regions and assign shipping methods based on customer location without implementing custom development. This limitation becomes a major challenge for businesses that operate in multiple cities, states, countries, or delivery regions. For example, a merchant may want to: Offer Local Delivery only within Delhi. Charge ₹50 for Delhi NCR. Charge ₹100 for nearby cities. Hide Local Delivery outside the service area. Offer Express Shipping only in selected states. Enable Cash on Delivery only for specific locations. Restrict delivery to selected postal codes. Currently, Bagisto does not provide a centralized mechanism to configure these location-based shipping rules. Instead, merchants must implement custom shipping carriers, custom validation logic, or third-party extensions, increasing development effort for functionality that is considered standard in modern eCommerce platforms. ### Describe the solution you'd like Introduce a native Shipping Zones module similar to WooCommerce. A Shipping Zone should represent a geographical delivery region where one or more shipping methods can be assigned. The module should allow administrators to create unlimited shipping zones and define matching rules based on multiple location levels. Location Matching Each Shipping Zone should support matching by: Country State / Province Region City ZIP / Postal Code ZIP / Postal Code ranges Wildcard postal codes Multiple postal codes Entire country Entire state Multiple states Multiple cities For example: Delhi Zone Country India State Delhi Cities New Delhi East Delhi South Delhi Postal Codes 110091 110092 110093 110096 Another example: South India Countries India States Tamil Nadu Kerala Karnataka Telangana Another example: International Countries USA Canada Australia This allows merchants to define service areas at different geographical levels depending on their business requirements. Shipping Methods per Zone Every Shipping Zone should support multiple shipping methods. For example: Delhi NCR Local Delivery Flat Rate Scheduled Delivery Free Shipping Mumbai Express Shipping Flat Rate Rest of India Standard Shipping International DHL FedEx UPS Only the shipping methods assigned to the customer's matching zone should be displayed during checkout. Zone Priority Shipping Zones should support priority ordering. When multiple zones match a customer's address, Bagisto should select the first matching zone. Example 1. East Delhi 2. Delhi 3. India 4. Rest of World A customer from postal code 110091 should match East Delhi rather than the broader Delhi or India zones. Postal Code Support Similar to WooCommerce, postal code matching should support: Individual postal codes 110091 110092 110096 Postal code ranges 122001...122018 Wildcard patterns 11009* 5600* This is especially useful for local delivery businesses that serve only selected neighborhoods. Shipping Classes Integration Similar to WooCommerce, Shipping Classes should work together with Shipping Zones. For example: Shipping Class Large Cake Small Cake Heavy Item Fragile Item Inside a Shipping Zone, merchants should be able to configure different shipping costs based on the assigned Shipping Class. Example: Delhi Zone Large Cake ₹100 Small Cake ₹50 Heavy Item ₹250 This provides much greater flexibility for calculating shipping charges. Suggested Admin Structure Configure └── Sales └── Shipping ├── Shipping Origin ├── Shipping Zones │ ├── Delhi NCR │ ├── Mumbai │ ├── South India │ └── Rest of World └── Shipping Classes ### Describe alternatives you've considered Currently, merchants must: Develop custom shipping packages. Override shipping carrier logic. Maintain separate delivery zone tables. Implement custom postcode validation. Configure shipping restrictions through custom code. Although these approaches are possible, they require significant development effort and duplicate functionality already available in other mature eCommerce platforms. A native Shipping Zone engine would provide a reusable foundation that all shipping methods and extensions could leverage. ### Additional context WooCommerce Reference WooCommerce provides one of the most flexible Shipping Zone implementations among open-source eCommerce platforms. Its Shipping Zone system allows merchants to: Create unlimited Shipping Zones. Match customer locations using: Country State Region City (via supported integrations) ZIP / Postal Codes ZIP ranges Wildcard ZIP patterns Assign multiple shipping methods to each zone. Configure different shipping rates based on Shipping Classes. Automatically display only the shipping methods available for the customer's location. Prioritize zones so that more specific regions take precedence over broader ones. This architecture makes shipping configuration simple while remaining highly scalable for businesses of all sizes. Benefits for Bagisto Adding native Shipping Zones would significantly strengthen Bagisto's shipping capabilities by providing: Native location-based shipping configuration. Reduced need for custom development. Better support for hyperlocal delivery businesses. Simplified configuration for merchants. Improved compatibility with third-party shipping providers. Better support for enterprise logistics. Easier implementation of delivery restrictions. Centralized management of shipping regions. Greater extensibility for future shipping modules. Business Use Cases This feature would benefit businesses such as: Grocery Delivery Cake & Bakery Stores Flower Delivery Pharmacy Delivery Food Delivery Furniture Stores Electronics Retailers Multi-city Retail Chains Hyperlocal Commerce Marketplace Platforms Multi-warehouse Operations Overall Impact A native Shipping Zone engine would establish a centralized geographic routing layer for Bagisto's shipping system. Rather than embedding location-specific conditions within individual shipping methods, merchants and developers could define reusable geographic rules once and associate any shipping method with them. This approach improves maintainability, reduces custom development, and aligns Bagisto with the capabilities offered by mature eCommerce platforms such as WooCommerce, making it more suitable for both local and enterprise-scale commerce.

title: [2.4] Product duplication does not copy Customizable Options
author: skm15187
body: ### Terms - [x] Before you openning this issue, i have checked if the issue has already been reported. ### Bagisto Version(s) affected 2.4.8 ### Issue Description When duplicating a product that contains Customizable Options, the duplicated product does not retain any of the configured customizable options. The Customizable Item section is empty in the duplicated product, even though the original product contains one or more options with their respective values. ### Preconditions Bagisto version: 2.4.7 also not in 2.4.8 A Simple Product exists with one or more Customizable Options configured (e.g., Select, Radio, Checkbox, etc.). The product is saved successfully. ### Steps to reproduce Go to Catalog → Products. Create or open a Simple Product. Add one or more Customizable Options with option values. Save the product. Click Duplicate for the product. Open the newly duplicated product. Navigate to the Customizable Item section. ### Expected Result All Customizable Options and their associated values should be copied to the duplicated product, including: Option title Option type Required/Optional setting Option values Price and price type SKU (if applicable) Sort order The duplicated product should behave exactly like the original. other ecommerce like opencart and woocommerce also doing same because it can save lot of time to create custom fields. ### Actual Result The product is duplicated successfully; however, the Customizable Item section is empty. None of the customizable options or their values are copied to the duplicated product. ### Screenshots _No response_ ### Additional context _No response_

title: Feature Request: Display only filter options that have associated products (Dynamic Faceted Filtering)
author: skm15187
body:
## Problem

Currently, Bagisto displays all predefined filter options on category pages, even when no products are associated with many of those options.

For example, if the "Recipient" attribute contains:

- Brother
- Sister
- Father
- Mother
- Son
- Daughter
- Boyfriend
- Girlfriend
- Grandfather

but the current category only contains products assigned to:

- Brother
- Sister
- Father

Bagisto still displays every option in the filter sidebar.

This creates several issues:

- Customers can select filters that have no matching products.
- The filter sidebar becomes unnecessarily long and cluttered.
- Mobile users must scroll through many irrelevant options.
- The shopping experience becomes confusing because selecting some filters appears to have no meaningful effect.

## Expected Behavior

Bagisto should implement dynamic faceted filtering similar to WooCommerce.

Only attribute options that are associated with at least one product in the current product collection should be displayed.

Example:

Instead of displaying:

- Brother
- Sister
- Father
- Mother
- Son
- Daughter
- Boyfriend
- Girlfriend

Display only:

- Brother (25)
- Sister (18)
- Father (9)

The numbers are optional but would be a useful enhancement.

If another category contains products for "Mother", then that option should automatically appear for that category.

This would provide a much cleaner UI, improve mobile usability, prevent users from selecting empty filters, and align Bagisto's filtering behavior with modern eCommerce platforms such as WooCommerce, Magento, and Shopify.

title: [2.4] Feature Request: Separate Mobile Category Logo and Mobile Category Banner Images
author: skm15187
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Yes.

Currently, Bagisto allows only one Logo image and one Category Banner image. The same image is used for both desktop and mobile devices.

This creates several issues:

- Desktop banner images are often cropped on mobile because of different aspect ratios.
- Important promotional text or product images become hidden on smaller screens.
- Merchants have to compromise by creating a single image that doesn't look optimal on either desktop or mobile.
- Store logos designed for desktop navigation often appear too small or unclear on mobile headers.

Many modern eCommerce platforms allow separate assets for desktop and mobile to provide a better user experience. This is especially important for mobile-first stores where most traffic comes from smartphones.

### Describe the solution you'd like

I would like Bagisto to support separate mobile-specific images.

### Store Branding

Add an optional field:

- Mobile Logo

Behavior:

- Desktop → Use Logo
- Mobile → Use Mobile Logo (if uploaded)
- Fallback → Use Desktop Logo

### Category

Add an optional field:

- Mobile Banner Image

Behavior:

- Desktop → Use Banner Image
- Mobile → Use Mobile Banner Image (if uploaded)
- Fallback → Use Desktop Banner Image

This implementation would be fully backward compatible because existing stores would continue using the current images unless mobile-specific images are uploaded.

This feature would significantly improve responsive design, allow merchants to optimize images for different screen sizes, and eliminate the need for custom theme modifications or CSS workarounds.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Currently, the only alternatives are:

- Creating a single banner image that tries to work for both desktop and mobile, which often results in poor composition and cropped content.
- Using CSS media queries to hide or replace images, which requires custom theme development and is not manageable from the admin panel.
- Maintaining separate custom theme overrides, making future Bagisto upgrades more difficult.
- Using image-safe zones, which limits design creativity and still doesn't provide an optimal experience on all screen sizes.

None of these approaches are ideal because they require additional development effort for a feature that could be managed directly from the Bagisto admin panel.

### Additional context

This feature would be particularly useful for stores that rely heavily on promotional banners, such as fashion, electronics, grocery, bakery, and gift stores.

Example:

Category: Birthday Cakes

Desktop Banner:
- 1920 × 700 (landscape)

Mobile Banner:
- 1080 × 1350 (portrait)

Using dedicated images for each device ensures better visual presentation, improved readability, and higher engagement.

Similarly, a dedicated Mobile Logo allows merchants to use a simplified or stacked logo that fits mobile headers without compromising branding.

The feature can be implemented in a fully backward-compatible way by making both "Mobile Logo" and "Mobile Banner Image" optional. If no mobile-specific image is uploaded, Bagisto can automatically fall back to the existing desktop image.

title: [2.4] Feature Enhancement: Admin Warning and Email Alert for Missing Queue Worker When QUEUE_CONNECTION is Not sync.
author: kartikeywebkul9260
body:
Here is an updated GitHub feature enhancement including the email notification idea:

# Feature Enhancement: Admin Warning and Email Alert for Missing Queue Worker

## Summary

Introduce a queue health monitoring mechanism that warns administrators when `QUEUE_CONNECTION` is configured with an asynchronous driver (e.g., `database`, `redis`) but no active queue worker is running.

Additionally, if any queue-related job is dispatched while the queue worker is unavailable, send an immediate non-queued email notification to the administrator informing them about the issue.

## Problem Statement

Bagisto uses queue workers to process background tasks such as sending emails, indexing, imports/exports, and other asynchronous operations.

When `QUEUE_CONNECTION` is not set to `sync` and the queue worker is not running, jobs continue accumulating in the queue and remain unprocessed. This may lead to:

- Emails not being sent.
- Background jobs remaining in pending state.
- Import/export processes getting stuck.
- Delayed or failed asynchronous operations.

Currently, administrators may not realize that the queue worker has stopped or was never started.

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Admin Panel Queue Health Warning

Add a health check that continuously verifies queue worker availability.

Display an admin warning such as:

> **Queue Worker Not Running**
>
> The application is configured to use the `{{ QUEUE_CONNECTION }}` queue driver, but no active queue worker has been detected. Background jobs may not be processed.
>
> Please start the queue worker using:
>
> `php artisan queue:work`

This warning should not be displayed when:

* `QUEUE_CONNECTION=sync`
* A healthy queue worker is detected.

### 2. Immediate Admin Email Notification (Non-Queued)

When an application attempts to dispatch a queue job and no active queue worker is detected:

* Send a direct email to the administrator without using the queue system.
* Include details such as:

  * Queue connection driver.
  * Job name or type that was dispatched.
  * Time of occurrence.
  * Server/environment details (optional).
  * Recommended action to restart the queue worker.

The email must be sent synchronously to avoid dependency on the failing queue system itself.

### 3. Prevent Repeated Notifications

To avoid excessive alerts:

* Store the last notification timestamp.
* Send the warning email only once within a configurable time period (e.g., every 30 or 60 minutes) until the queue worker becomes healthy again.

## Benefits

* Administrators receive immediate visibility of queue failures.
* Prevents unnoticed delays in critical background operations.
* Reduces troubleshooting time.
* Improves reliability of email, import/export, and other asynchronous features.
* Avoids notification loops by implementing throttling.

## Expected Outcome

Whenever the system is configured to use asynchronous queues but no queue worker is available, administrators receive clear warnings in the admin panel and timely email alerts, allowing them to quickly restore queue processing.

title: security: v-html used with unsanitized user data in Shop views — XSS pattern fixed for customer_name but remains in product_name and datagrid columns
author: kapil971390
body:
## Summary Commit `e28db3e` fixed an XSS vulnerability by replacing `v-html="record.customer_name"` with `@{{ record.customer_name }}` in `create.blade.php`. The same `v-html` pattern still exists in several other Shop views with data that can contain user-supplied or seller-supplied content. ## Remaining v-html with user-data **1. Downloadable products — product name rendered as HTML:** ``` packages/Webkul/Shop/src/Resources/views/customers/account/downloadable_products/index.blade.php:89 v-html="record.product_name" ``` Product names are set by admins or marketplace sellers. In a multi-vendor setup, a seller can inject arbitrary HTML/JS into their product name and it will execute in every buyer's browser on the downloadable products page. **2. Datagrid table component — blanket v-html for all columns:** ``` packages/Webkul/Shop/src/Resources/views/components/datagrid/table.blade.php:134 v-html="record[column.index]" ``` This renders **every column value** of every datagrid as raw HTML. Any column that contains user-supplied data (order titles, RMA titles, method names) becomes an XSS vector. **3. RMA views — order ID, title, quantities:** ``` packages/Webkul/Shop/src/Resources/views/customers/account/rma/index.blade.php:85,98,108 v-html="record.order_id" v-html="record.title" v-html="record.total_quantity" ``` ## What Was Fixed (for reference) In `e28db3e`, `create.blade.php` was changed from: ```html

``` to: ```html

@{{ record.customer_name }}

``` The same change is needed for the fields listed above. Vue's `{{ }}` syntax HTML-escapes values automatically; `v-html` bypasses this protection entirely. ## Suggested Fix Replace `v-html="record.X"` with `{{ record.X }}` for all fields that don't intentionally contain trusted server-rendered HTML (like `price_html`). For the blanket datagrid component (`v-html="record[column.index]"`), add a per-column `raw_html` flag so only explicitly trusted columns render as HTML. ## Files - `packages/Webkul/Shop/src/Resources/views/customers/account/downloadable_products/index.blade.php` line 89 - `packages/Webkul/Shop/src/Resources/views/components/datagrid/table.blade.php` line 134 - `packages/Webkul/Shop/src/Resources/views/customers/account/rma/index.blade.php` lines 85, 98, 108

title: Add native "Pago Móvil" (Venezuela) payment method with manual verification
author: jregnaultt
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Currently, Bagisto lacks a native or easily configurable payment method tailored for the Venezuelan market, specifically for "Pago Móvil" (an instant interbank mobile payment system).

Right now, store owners in Venezuela have to repurpose the standard "Money Transfer" or "Bank Transfer" methods. However, these generic methods do not provide specific fields to collect the unique data required to verify a Pago Móvil transaction, leading to order processing delays and friction between the merchant and the customer.

### Describe the solution you'd like

I would like to see a dedicated, native "Pago Móvil" payment method added to Bagisto's core (or as a standard payment package). It should operate as a semi-automated/manual verification gateway with the following workflow:

1. Admin Configuration (Store Owner):
In the admin backend (Configuration > Sales > Payment Methods), the owner should be able to enable "Pago Móvil" and fill in their receiving credentials:

- Bank Name (Dropdown or text field)
- ID/RIF Number (e.g., V-12345678)
- Phone Number associated with the bank account.

2. Customer Checkout Experience:

- During checkout, the customer selects "Pago Móvil" as the payment method.
- The system displays the merchant's Pago Móvil details (Bank, ID, Phone) so the customer can open their banking app and make the transfer.
- Post-Order Payment Proof: After placing the order (or during the final step), the customer must be prompted to input their transaction details for verification:
        * Origin Bank (The bank they paid from)
        * Reference/Operation Number (The receipt number)
        * Holder's ID/Phone (Optional, but useful for tracking)

3. Admin Order Management:

- The order status is set to "Pending Payment".
- The admin can see the customer's submitted reference number and bank details inside the order view to manually cross-reference their bank account and approve the payment.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

title: Venezuelan Bolivar doesn't update in exchange rates.
author: jregnaultt
body: ### Terms - [x] Before you openning this issue, i have checked if the issue has already been reported. ### Bagisto Version(s) affected 2.4 ### Issue Description When using the automatic exchange rate synchronization features in Bagisto, the Venezuelan Bolivar exchange rate does not fetch or calculate correctly, remaining stuck at a flat value of 1.0000000000. This occurs despite having a valid third-party service like Exchange Rates API properly configured and activated. ### Preconditions Currency Venezuelan Bolivar is created and active in the system. A valid API Key for Exchange Rates API (or Fixer API) is configured under Configuration > General > Exchange Rates. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Go to Settings > Currencies and ensure Venezuelan Bolivar is listed with the proper currency code. 2. Navigate to Configuration > General > Exchange Rates and confirm that Exchange Rates API is selected as the default service and contains a valid API key. 3. Go to Settings > Exchange Rates and click on the "Update Exchange Rate" button. 4. Check the updated value for Venezuelan Bolivar. ### Expected Result The system should fetch the live, accurate market exchange rate relative to the base currency from the configured API and update the "Exchanges rate" field accordingly. ### Actual Result While other currencies (like the Euro) update properly to their relative fractional/decimal rates, the Venezuelan Bolivar remains hard-stuck at 1.0000000000 ### Screenshots ### Additional context _No response_

title: Fix WCAG accessibility issues (keyboard navigation, semantic buttons, screen reader visibility) & missing translations
author: SopsoN
body:
### Terms

- [x] Before you openning this issue, i have checked if the issue has already been reported.

### Bagisto Version(s) affected

2.4.0

### Issue Description

Several interactive elements across the storefront (mini-cart, checkout, image zoomer, tabs, modals, pagination) use non-semantic HTML tags like <span> or <div> with @click events instead of native <button type="button"> tags. This breaks standard keyboard navigation.

Additionally, many form inputs (like checkboxes and radios) use the hidden class, making them completely invisible to screen readers. Finally, focus indicators for keyboard users are missing or inconsistent across the UI, and the 'stars' translation key is missing across all language files for product reviews.

### Preconditions

Fresh installation of Bagisto 2.4.0.

Basic storefront configuration.

Attempting to navigate the shop strictly using a keyboard (Tab / Shift+Tab) or a screen reader.

### Steps to reproduce

Go to the storefront.

Press Tab to navigate through interactive elements like the mini-cart, currency/locale switchers, product tabs, or checkout steps.

Try to open the image zoomer gallery or navigate the carousel using only the keyboard.

Inspect custom radio buttons and checkboxes (e.g., in checkout).

### Expected Result

All interactive elements should be reachable via the Tab key and have a clear focus state (focus-visible).

Interactive elements should be native <button> tags for proper semantic HTML.

Hidden inputs (like custom checkboxes/radios) should use sr-only instead of hidden so screen readers can interpret them.

### Actual Result

Focus states are missing on many elements.

<span> and <div> are used for clickable elements, preventing proper keyboard interaction.

Inputs with the hidden class are completely ignored by screen readers.

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Additional context

I have already prepared a PR that refactors these elements to use native <button> tags, replaces .hidden with .sr-only on inputs, adds Tailwind's focus-visible: states, and adds the missing 'stars' translation keys.

Request a solution

title: feat(configurable-variant-guard): scoped variant image save for configurable products
author: celpoeta
body:
### Pre-flight Checks
- [x] I have searched existing issues and this is not a duplicate
- [x] I understand this issue needs status:approved before a PR can be opened

### Problem Description
Configurable product variant images are currently too expensive to persist through the parent product form when many variants are selected. Even after moving away from the monolithic parent submit, large multi-variant image operations can still fail without clear feedback if we do not handle the boundary carefully.

The package now needs a dedicated scoped image-save flow for persisted variants so variant media can be saved independently of the parent product submit, with clear progress/error feedback and payload control.

### Proposed Solution
Implement scoped variant image persistence entirely inside packages/Webkul/ConfigurableVariantGuard/ without touching Bagisto core.

The solution should:
- add dedicated admin endpoints for single and batch variant image operations
- persist variant images immediately via AJAX for persisted variants
- keep inline-created variants on parent-form fallback
- chunk batch uploads to avoid oversized requests
- enforce 1 MB max image size and require external compression workflow
- show global persistent Bagisto feedback while images are saving, then success/error on completion
- keep scalar mass edits (price, SKU, status, etc.) on the current parent-save flow

### Affected Area
Other

### Alternatives Considered
- Raising infra limits only: rejected as primary solution because it hides the architectural problem
- Moving everything to async jobs immediately: deferred; synchronous chunked AJAX is simpler and sufficient for now
- Touching core Bagisto configurable save flow: rejected by project constraint

title: [2.3] Refund Exception When Order is Placed Before PayPal Smart Button UI Loads
author: aviral-webkul
body: ### Terms - [x] Before you openning this issue, i have checked if the issue has already been reported. ### Bagisto Version(s) affected 2.4, 2.3 ### Issue Description When a customer selects the PayPal Smart Button payment method during checkout and immediately clicks the “Place Order” button before the PayPal Smart Button UI fully loads, the order gets created successfully but later throws an exception during refund creation. Error Message: Trying to access array offset on value of type null ### Preconditions 1. PayPal Smart Button payment method is enabled and configured. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Enable PayPal Smart Button payment method. 2. Go to storefront checkout. 3. Select “PayPal Smart Button” as payment method. 4. Immediately click “Place Order” before the PayPal Smart Button UI finishes loading/rendering. 5. Order gets created successfully. 6. Go to Admin Panel → Sales → Orders. 7. Open the created order. 8. Try to create a refund. 9. Exception appears. ### Expected Result 1. Order placement should be blocked until PayPal Smart Button is fully initialized. ### Actual Result 1. Order is created with missing PayPal payment additional data. 2. Refund creation crashes with null offset exception. ### Screenshots ### Additional context _No response_
